question: A Windows host was triaged using Amcache forensics. What executable running from C:\Temp is recorded in the Amcache data? Use the get_amcache tool and answer with the executable filename only.
Using Amcache evidence, what was the last (most recently recorded) program on this Windows host? Use the get_amcache tool and answer with the executable filename only.
How many program execution records are in the Amcache artifact for this case? Use the get_amcache tool and answer with the integer count only.
What is the hour and minute of the earliest recorded execution in the Amcache? Use the get_amcache tool and answer in HH:MM format.
What Windows calculator application appears in the Amcache records? Use the get_amcache tool and answer with the executable filename only.
UserAssist records show a suspicious executable was launched interactively from C:\Temp. What is its filename? Use the get_userassist tool and answer with the filename only.
What document file was opened by the user according to the UserAssist forensic artifact? Use the get_userassist tool and answer with the filename only.
According to UserAssist records, what was the most recently accessed item on this system? Use the get_userassist tool and answer with the filename only.
How many GUI-launched items are recorded in the UserAssist artifact for this case? Use the get_userassist tool and answer with the integer count only.
What graphics painting application was launched by the user according to UserAssist data? Use the get_userassist tool and answer with the executable filename only.
The Background Activity Moderator (BAM) artifact shows a persistence-related executable. What is its filename? Use the get_bam tool and answer with the filename only.
A suspicious executable in AppData\Local\Temp was recorded by BAM. What is its filename? Use the get_bam tool and answer with the filename only.
How many background processes are recorded in the BAM artifact for this case? Use the get_bam tool and answer with the integer count only.
What executable from C:\Temp was recorded in the BAM (Background Activity Moderator) artifact? Use the get_bam tool and answer with the filename only.
What was the first (earliest timestamp) background process recorded by BAM in this case? Use the get_bam tool and answer with the executable filename only.
Sysmon process creation logs show a command-and-control agent was executed from C:\Temp. What is its filename? Use the get_sysmon_4688 tool and answer with the filename only.
What Windows reconnaissance tool was executed on this host according to Sysmon records? Use the get_sysmon_4688 tool and answer with the executable filename only.
What scripting engine execution was recorded by Sysmon on this host? Use the get_sysmon_4688 tool and answer with the executable filename only.
How many process creation events are recorded in the Sysmon logs for this case? Use the get_sysmon_4688 tool and answer with the integer count only.
A suspicious executable from C:\Temp appears in Sysmon event logs. What is its filename? Use the get_sysmon_4688 tool and answer with the filename only.
Prefetch forensic evidence shows a credential dumping tool was executed on this host. What is its filename? Use the get_prefetch tool and answer with the executable filename only.
What lateral movement and remote execution tool appears in the Prefetch records for this host? Use the get_prefetch tool and answer with the executable filename only.
How many Prefetch entries are recorded for this Windows host? Use the get_prefetch tool and answer with the integer count only.
What executable from C:\Temp appears in the Prefetch artifact? There may be more than one — list all of them. Use the get_prefetch tool.
What Windows networking utility appears in the Prefetch execution records? Use the get_prefetch tool and answer with the executable filename only.
